* feat: add authenticated E2E tests for full organizer-participant booking flow

Add Playwright auth setup with seed accounts and a full happy-path spec:
organizer creates/publishes event → participant books → self-checks-in →
organizer verifies check-in on dashboard. Also fix pre-existing failures
in auth, home, and navigation specs (strict mode violations, splash screen overlay).

Organizers can now add participants directly from the event dashboard,
either by searching existing users or entering a name+contact for walk-ins.
Includes inline status dropdown, RLS policy for organizer inserts, and
migration for new booking columns (added_by, manual_status, manual_name, manual_contact).

* fix: organizer auth check — compare against organizer_profiles.id, not users.id

events.organizer_id references organizer_profiles.id, so the auth check
was always failing. Now looks up the organizer profile by user_id first.

fix: split CI workflow so check names match branch protection (#214)

* fix: split CI workflow so check names match branch protection

ci.yml had both `pull_request` and `workflow_call` triggers, causing
GitHub to append "(pull_request)" to check names. Branch protection
expects `CI / Lint`, `CI / Build`, `CI / Test` but got
`CI / Lint (pull_request)`, etc., forcing bypass on every merge.

Split into ci-shared.yml (reusable, workflow_call only) and ci.yml
(PR trigger only, calls ci-shared.yml). Single trigger = no suffix.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: address review feedback — improve CLAUDE.md readability

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: define CI jobs directly in ci.yml instead of calling reusable workflow

Reusable workflow calls prefix check names with the calling job ID
(e.g. "ci / Lint") instead of the workflow name ("CI / Lint"). Define
the jobs directly in ci.yml with pull_request-only trigger to produce
the exact check names branch protection expects.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: pass correct check names to auto-approve workflow

The checks API returns bare job names (Lint, Build, Test) not the
composite names (CI / Lint, etc.) that the shared workflow defaults to.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: use uppercase CI job ID to produce correct check names

GitHub Actions names reusable workflow checks as
<calling_job_id> / <reusable_job_id>. Using CI (uppercase) as the
calling job produces CI / Lint, CI / Build, CI / Test — matching
branch protection requirements exactly.
